modules.title: Stones, Brick, Mortar and Concrete
topics: Stones – Occurrence, Classification of Rocks, varieties, Characteristics and their testing, uses, quarrying and dressing of stones, Deterioration of Stones, Retardation of Decay of Stones, Preservation of Stones, Artificial Stones.
Brick – Manufacturing, characteristics, Classification and uses, Improved brick from inferior soils, Hand molding brick table, Clay‑fly ash brick table.
Concrete – Ingredients, Grades of Concrete, Concrete Production, Special Concrete.

modules.title: Timber, Glass, Steel and Aluminium
topics: Timber – Important timbers, their engineering properties and uses, defects in timber, seasoning and treatment, need for wood substitutes, Plywood, Particle Board, Fibre Board, Applications of wood and wood products, Plaster Boards, Adhesives, types of Gypsum Board and their uses.
Glass – What is glass, Nature of glass, Structure of glass, Macro molecular structure, Main oxides in glass, Thermal and optical properties, Effect of coating.
Steel – Physical properties of structural steel, Grades of steel.

modules.title: Flooring, Roofing, Plumbing and Sanitary Material
topics: Flooring and Roofing tiles, Types of flooring – Marble, Kota stone, wood etc., Type of roofing, P.V.C. materials, CI, GI, Asbestos pipe, Stone ware pipes.

modules.title: Paints, Enamels and Varnishes
topics: Composition of oil paint, characteristic of an ideal paint, preparation of paint, covering power of paints, Painting – Plastered surfaces, painting wood surfaces, painting metal surfaces; Defects, Effect of weather, enamels, distemper, water wash and colour wash, Varnish, French polish, Wax polish.

modules.title: Miscellaneous Construction Materials
topics: Bitumen, Tar and Asphalt – their characteristics and uses, Ultra Poly Vinyl chloride pipes, Thermal and sound insulating materials, water proofing materials.

reference_books: Donald R Askeland, Pradeep P Fulay, Wendelin J Wright, The Science and Engineering of Materials, Cengage Learning.
S K Duggal, Building Materials, New Age International.
P C Vaghese, Building Materials, PHI Learning.
S.C. Rangwala, Engineering Materials, Charotar.
M S Shetty, Concrete Technology, S. Chand Technical.
A M Neville, J J Brooks, Concrete Technology, Prentice Hall.

modules.title: Tacheometry
topics: Use of Tacheometry for Traversing and Contouring

experiments: Determination of tachometric constants & uses of tachometer in various field works
reference_books: B.C Punmia, Surveying Vol-II & III, Laxmi Publication.
S.K. Duggal, Surveying Vol. II McGraw Hill Publishing Company Ltd.
Saikia MD, Das BM, Das MM, Surveying, McGraw hill.
T.P. Kanetkar and S.V. Kulkarini, Surveying and Leveling-Part-I & II, Pune VidyarthiGrihaPrakashan, Pune.
Gopi A, Satikumar R, Advance surveying, Pearson.
Remote Sensing and image interpretation by Lillesand T.M. and Kiefer R.W.
R. Agor, Advance Surveying, Khanna Publisher.
Chandra AM, Higher Surveying, New Age International, new Dwlhi.
Bhavikatti SS, Surveying and Levelling Vol. II, I.K International.
Venkatramaiah, Surveying, University Press, Mumbai.
Bhatta Basudeb, Remote Sensing and GIS, Oxford, New Delhi.
Subramanaian, Surveying & levelling, Oxford, New Delhi.
Joseph George, Fundamentals of Remote Sensing.

modules.title: Drawing of Building Elements
topics: Various types of footings (open foundation, raft, grillage, pile, well foundation)
Various types of doors, windows and ventilators

modules.title: Building Planning
topics: Sketch plans and working drawings for residential, institutional and commercial buildings
Introduction to computer‑aided design and drafting (CAD)
Principles of architectural composition (unity, contrast, etc.)

modules.title: Building Services
topics: Elevators and escalators – standards and uses

modules.title: Principles of Architectural Design
topics: Definition of architecture and factors influencing architectural development
Characteristics and features of architectural styles, historic examples, creative principles
Principles of architectural composition – unity, balance, proportion, scale, rhythm, harmony, accentuation, contrast
Organising principles – symmetry, hierarchy, axis, linear, concentric, radial, asymmetric grouping, primary and secondary masses, role of colour, texture, shapes/forms
Architectural space and mass – visual and emotional effects of geometric forms, space activity, tolerance space
Forms related to materials and structural systems

Elements of architecture – functions (pragmatic utility, circulatory, symbolic, physiological), structure (physical, perceptual), space (positive, negative), aesthetics (visual perception), protection (climate protection), comfort factors

modules.title: Perspective Drawing and Town Planning
topics: Elements of perspective drawing – simple problems, one‑point and two‑point perspectives
Master plan concepts, structure plan, detailed town‑planning scheme, action plan
Estimating future needs – planning standards for different land uses
Allocation for commerce, industries, public amenities, open areas
Planning standards for density distributions and density zones
Planning standards for traffic network – road and path standards
Provision for urban growth, growth models, plan implementation
Urban financing, land acquisition, slum‑clearance schemes

experiments: Drawing of various building components containing doors, windows ventilators, lintels and arches stairs foundations etc.
Drawings for services and interiors of buildings.
Drawings containing detailed planning of one/two bed room residential building (common to all student)
Drawing of residential and institutional building (Each student performs a different drawing).
Use of Auto CAD for preparation of drawings.
reference_books: Shah, Kale & Patki; Building Design and Drawing; TMH
Malik & Meo; Building Design and Drawing
W B Mckay, Orient Blackswan Building Construction Vol 1 -4, Pearson
Gurucharan Singh and Jagdish Singh, Building Planning, Designing and Scheduling, Standard Publishers Distributors.
Layal JS, Dongre A, Building Design and Drawing, Satya Prakashan
Ghose D.N., Civil Engineering Design and Drawing, CBS publisher
Das B M, Principles of Foundation Engineering, Cengage Learning.
Agrawal S. C., Architecture and Town Planning, Dhanpat Rai & Co.
S.C. Rangwala, Town Planning, Charotar Publishing House.
Lewis Keeble, Principles and Practice of Town and Country Planning.
Rame Gouda, Principles & Practices of Town Planning, University of Mysore, Manasa Gangotri.

modules.title: Simple Stress and Strains
topics: Concept of elastic body stress and strain
Various types of stress and strains
Stresses in compound bars, composite and tapering bars
Complex stress and strains – two‑dimensional and three‑dimensional stress system

modules.title: Bending and Shearing Stresses
topics: Concept of pure bending and bending stress
Differential equation of the elastic curve
Determination of bending stresses in simply supported, cantilever and overhanging beams subjected to point load and uniformly distributed load
Bending stress distribution across beam cross‑section
Shearing stress distribution across beam cross‑section

modules.title: Determination of Slope and Deflection of Beams
topics: Double integration method for slope and deflection

modules.title: Columns and Struts
topics: Theory of columns and slenderness ratio
Direct and bending stresses in short columns
Euler’s buckling load for various end conditions
Thin pressure vessels: cylinders and spheres
Change in diameter and volume of vessels
Theories of failure for pressure vessels

modules.title: Torsion of Shafts
topics: Concept of pure torsion and torsion equation
Determination of shear stress and angle of twist for solid circular shafts
Determination of shear stress and angle of twist for hollow circular shafts
Analysis of combined bending and torsion problems
Unsymmetrical bending: principal moment of inertia and product of inertia
Bending of beams not in a plane of symmetry
Pure bending of curved beams (rectangular, circular, trapezoidal sections)
Stress distribution and neutral axis in curved beams

experiments: Determine the compressive and tensile strength of materials
Determine the Brinell hardness of materials
Determine the Rockwell hardness of materials
Determine the toughness of the materials
Determine the stiffness of the spring
Determine the deflection of beam using a deflection‑beam apparatus
reference_books: Punmia B.C., Mechanics of Materials, Laxmi Publications (P) Ltd.
S.S Bhavikaati, Strength of Materials, Vikas Publisher, New Delhi
Rajput R. K., Strength of Materials, S. Chand.
S. Ramamrutham, R. Narayanan, Strength of Materials, DhanpatRai Publications.
R. Subramaniam, Strength of Materials, Oxford University Press.
Sadhu Singh, Strength of Material, Khanna Publishers
Mubeen A, Mechanics of solids, Pearsons
D.S Prakash Rao, Strength of Material, University Press, Hyderabad
Debrath Nag, Strength of Material, Wiley
Jindal, Strength of Material, Pearsons.
Bansal R.K, Strength of Materials, Laxmi Publisher, New Delhi.
Nash, W.A., Strength of Materials, Mcgraw hills, New Delhi.
Chandramouli, Strength of Materials, PHI learning
Dongre A.P., Strength of Materials, Scitech, Chennai
Negi L. S, Strength of Materials, McGraw Hill Professional.
Raj Puroshattam, Strength of Material, Pearsons
J.M. Gere, J. G. Barry Mechanics of Material, Cengage Learning

subjects.subject_name: Study of Historical & Ancient Civil Engineering Practices

modules.title: General Study of ancient monuments e.g. Forts, Bridges, Buildings and various other civil engineering related structures
topics: Overview of ancient monuments including forts, bridges, buildings and related civil engineering structures

modules.title: Environmental practices adopted in construction of historical structure during ancient/medieval period
topics: Study of environmental practices applied in ancient and medieval construction

modules.title: Construction techniques and materials used in historical structures
topics: Investigation of construction techniques and materials employed in historical structures

modules.title: Various planning aspects adopted in historical structures
topics: Analysis of planning aspects in historical civil engineering works

modules.title: Visit of various historical structures and museums to understand history of civil engineering practices
topics: Field visits to historical structures and museums for learning civil engineering history

experiments: Detailed study report on various aspects such as environmental practices, construction techniques and materials, planning etc. of one important ancient structure, including relevant sketches/drawings, and presentation before departmental committee.
